app.name: LogicStamp Context
app.one_liner: A CLI that compiles TypeScript codebases into deterministic, diffable architectural contracts and dependency graphs for AI coding workflows.

screens.name: Main Interface
screens.purpose: Display project context status, contract generation progress, and watch mode violations.

rust_commands.name: generate_contracts
rust_commands.purpose: Compile TypeScript codebase into deterministic .uif.json architectural contracts using AST extraction.

rust_commands.name: serve_mcp_server
rust_commands.purpose: Launch an MCP server to provide structured context to AI agents like Claude, Cursor, and Copilot Chat.

rust_commands.name: start_watch_mode
rust_commands.purpose: Initiate strict watch mode that compares current state against a git-like baseline and reports breaking changes.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build lean-style contract bundling with caching
week 1: Implement AST-based contract extraction from TypeScript files
week 1: Integrate AJV schema validation for .uif.json files
week 2: Add atomic file writes and signal handlers for crash safety
week 2: Develop strict watch mode with baseline diffing
week 2: Expose contracts via embedded MCP server
acceptance_tests: Given a TypeScript codebase, when contracts are generated in lean style mode, then output .uif.json files must be valid against the published JSON schema.
If a .uif.json contract fails AJV validation at load time, the system must reject it and not propagate invalid data to AI agents.
When the MCP server is running, it must respond to tool-use requests from compatible AI agents with up-to-date architectural context.
When watch mode is active and a breaking change is introduced, then the system must report current state vs baseline and write violations to disk atomically.
